sections.paragraph: ἀρετή, ῆς, ἡ [cp. ἀρέσκω; ‘uncommon excellence/capability’] a fundamental term in Hellenic society relating to intrinsic personal worth exhibited in character traits or civic performance meriting praise and affirmation, hence esp. of military prowess in Homeric society and then of a broader range of personal characteristics beneficial to society, excellence, virtue Phil 4:8; 1 Pt 2:9; 2 Pt 1:3, 5. In 1 Pt 2:9 the focus is on demonstrations of ἀρετή; in the others the abstract idea of praiseworthiness and concrete exhibition of ἀ. coalesce.
